title: Healthcare: Diagnostics & Safety
concept: Computer vision in healthcare assists doctors in diagnostic imaging, detects surgical tools, and monitors patient safety.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is occlusion in computer vision?

options.text: When an object of interest is partially or fully hidden by another object
When a model's weights are quantized to INT8
When a network undergoes knowledge distillation

exercises.explain: Occlusion refers to the blocking of visual paths between the camera and the target object.

cards.title: An AI assistant for doctors
cards.body: A radiologist might look at hundreds of X-rays a day, searching for tiny signs of illness. Under fatigue, humans can make mistakes. Computer vision acts as a tireless co-pilot, flagging abnormalities in seconds so doctors can focus their expertise!

cards.title: Computer Vision in Healthcare
cards.body: Healthcare vision applications include medical image classification (detecting tumors), anatomical segmentation (outlining organs in MRI scans), and surgical monitoring (tracking instruments and surgical safety).

cards.title: A high-powered digital highlighter
cards.body: Think of medical segmentation like a smart highlighter pen. Instead of a doctor spending hours manually coloring in the boundaries of a brain tumor, the AI highlights the exact region instantly, allowing for precise radiation targeting.

cards.title: MRI tumor segmenter
cards.body: A hospital deploys a U-Net segmentation model to analyze 3D brain scans. The model outlines tumor volumes in minutes, helping surgeons plan surgeries with sub-millimeter precision.

cards.title: AI does not replace the doctor
cards.body: You might think AI is ready to diagnose patients alone. In reality, healthcare models act as clinical decision support. Because false negatives can be fatal, final diagnoses are always confirmed by human physicians.

left.text:
Detects presence of disease
e.g., Pneumonia vs. Normal
Binary or multi-class label
Guides initial screening

right.text:
Traces boundary of structure
e.g., Outlining organ pixels
Pixel-level masks
Used for surgical planning

exercises.prompt: Which network architecture style is commonly used for medical image segmentation tasks?
exercises.explain: U-Net is the standard architecture for medical segmentation due to its skip connections that retain spatial details.

exercises.prompt: True or False: In healthcare, a false negative (missing a tumor) is usually considered far more critical than a false positive.
exercises.explain: True. Missing a disease (false negative) can delay life-saving treatment, whereas a false positive can be corrected during subsequent reviews.

exercises.sentence: Tracing the exact boundary of a kidney in a CT scan is a medical ___ task.
exercises.explain: Tracing boundaries at the pixel level is image segmentation.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary role of computer vision models in modern hospitals?

options.text: To act as diagnostic assistants to radiologists and surgeons
To automatically write prescription medication dosages
To schedule staff shifts and clean hospital rooms

exercises.explain: Vision models assist medical professionals by screening scans and highlighting abnormal regions.

exercises.prompt: You are training an AI model to detect skin cancer from photos. You want to ensure the model catches every possible case, even if it leads to extra doctor visits. Which metric should you optimize?
exercises.explain: In medical screening, high sensitivity (recall) ensures that the model catches as many true positive cases of disease as possible, keeping patient safety as the top priority.
